UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information, linked to their external authentication account. Stores details like contact information for faster checkout and order history.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This ID should correspond to the user ID from the external authentication system.

email.description: The user's email address, typically obtained from the external authentication system.

fullName.description: The user's full name, used for order processing and display in the account dashboard.

phoneNumber.description: The user's default phone number for contact and order processing, saved for convenience.

address.description: The user's default shipping/billing address, saved for faster checkout.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a category for grouping products, such as 'PlayStation Redeem Codes' or 'Xbox Gift Cards'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., 'PlayStation Redeem Codes').

description.description: A brief description of what this category includes, used for informational purposes.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the category, used for UI display in navigation or listings.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a digital game redeem code or a gaming product available for sale on Castro Nepal.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the product as displayed to customers.

price.description: The current selling price of the product.

shortDescription.description: A concise summary or tagline for the product, displayed in product listings.

fullDescription.description: A detailed and comprehensive description of the product, shown on the product details page.

mainImageUrl.description: URL to the primary image representing the product.

additionalImageUrls.description: An array of URLs to additional images or screenshots for the product.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

stockStatus.description: Indicates the general availability status of the product (e.g., 'In Stock', 'Out of Stock', 'Limited Stock').

quantityAvailable.description: The current number of units of this product available in stock.

customerInstructions.description: Specific instructions or guidelines for the customer on how to use or redeem the product, typically for digital codes.

isFeatured.description: A flag indicating if this product should be highlighted as a featured item on the homepage.

isTrending.description: A flag indicating if this product is currently popular or trending, used for display in trending sections.

releaseDate.description: The date and time when the product was initially released or added to the store, used for 'Recently Added' features.

relatedProductIds.description: An array of Product IDs that are considered related or similar to this product, for recommendations. (Relationship: Product N:N Product)

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's purchase order, containing details about the customer and the items purchased.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed this order. This is optional to support guest checkouts. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

customerName.description: The full name of the customer as provided during checkout for this specific order.

customerPhoneNumber.description: The phone number of the customer as provided during checkout for this specific order.

customerAddress.description: The shipping/billing address of the customer as provided during checkout for this specific order.

customerEmail.description: The email address of the customer as provided during checkout for this specific order.

notes.description: Any additional messages or special instructions provided by the customer for this order.

orderTime.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

totalPrice.description: The total monetary value of all items in the order, including any taxes or fees (if applicable).

status.description: The current processing status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

discordWebhookSent.description: A flag indicating whether the order details have been successfully sent to the Discord webhook.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single product item within an order, detailing the product purchased, its quantity, and price at the time of purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was purchased. This links to the product's current catalog entry.

productName.description: The name of the product at the time the order was placed (denormalized for historical accuracy).

quantity.description: The quantity of the product purchased in this order item.

unitPrice.description: The price of one unit of the product at the time the order was placed (denormalized for historical accuracy).

subtotal.description: The total price for this specific order item (quantity * unitPrice).

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Represents an announcement created by an administrator, displayed on the homepage to inform users about updates, discounts, or new products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Announcement entity.

title.description: The title or headline of the announcement.

content.description: The main message or body of the announcement.

publicationDate.description: The date and time when the announcement was published and became visible.

expiryDate.description: The date and time when the announcement should no longer be displayed. This field is optional.

isActive.description: A flag indicating whether the announcement is currently active and should be displayed on the homepage.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Each document's ID corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching {userId}. The document must contain a 'id' field that matches the {userId} path parameter for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores individual orders placed by registered users. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching {userId} or administrators. The document must contain a 'userId' field that matches the {userId} path parameter for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed the order, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific order.

definition.description: Stores individual items within an order placed by a registered user. Access inherits from the parent order: restricted to the authenticated user matching {userId} or administrators. The 'orderId' field within the document must match the {orderId} path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user associated with the parent order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific item within the order.

definition.description: Stores orders placed by guest users (those without a Firebase Authentication UID). This collection is accessible only by administrators for management. It is structurally segregated to ensure different security postures from user-owned orders.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific guest order.

definition.description: Stores individual items within an order placed by a guest user. This subcollection is accessible only by administrators, inheriting authorization from the parent guest order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent guest order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific item within the guest order.

definition.description: Stores product categories. Documents are publicly readable by all users (authenticated and unauthenticated) for product browsing. Only administrators can create, update, or delete categories. Admin role check is performed via the /roles_admin collection.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product category.

definition.description: Stores individual product listings. Documents are publicly readable by all users. Only administrators can create, update, or delete products. Admin role check is performed via the /roles_admin collection.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

definition.description: Stores system-wide announcements. Documents are publicly readable by all users for informational purposes. Only administrators can create, update, or delete announcements. Admin role check is performed via the /roles_admin collection.

params.name: announcementId
params.description: The unique identifier for the announcement.

definition.description: A collection used for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC) to identify administrators. The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{request.auth.uid}` grants administrator privileges. This relies on an 'existence check' in security rules for authorization independence.

params.name: uid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of a user designated as an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES. It leverages path-based authorization, structural segregation, and minimal denormalization to achieve Authorization Independence and enable secure QAPs.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **User-Owned Data:** User-specific data like `UserProfile` and `Orders` are structured under `/users/{userId}/...`. This design ensures that authorization rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to parent documents or external checks for ownership. For instance, a user's `Order` document, though nested, implicitly carries the `userId` in its path, making authorization atomic and independent.
2.  **Public & Admin Data:** Collections like `/categories`, `/products`, and `/announcements` are designed for public read access. Write access to these, and all access to `/guest_orders`, is restricted to administrators. Admin status is determined by the existence of a document in the `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection. This `exists()` check is a constant-time operation and does not involve `get()` calls that would break authorization independence.
3.  **Nested Data:** `OrderItem` documents are nested under specific orders (`/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}/order_items/{orderItemId}` or `/guest_orders/{orderId}/order_items/{orderItemId}`). Authorization for these items is implicitly handled by the parent path segments. Rules for `order_items` can directly use the `{userId}` or `{orderId}` wildcards from their parent paths without needing to retrieve the actual parent `Order` document. This avoids hierarchical dependencies.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) Support:**
1.  **User-Specific Lists:** For data under `/users/{userId}`, `list` operations are naturally secure. A user can only query their own path (`/users/{request.auth.uid}/orders`), ensuring that the returned results are inherently authorized and do not require further client-side filtering.
2.  **Public Lists:** Collections like `/categories`, `/products`, and `/announcements` are designed for global public read access. Firestore rules will permit `list` operations for all users, returning a complete and authorized dataset without any filtering. This perfectly aligns with QAPs, as the collection's security posture is homogeneous.
3.  **Admin-Only Lists:** The `/guest_orders` and `/roles_admin` collections are accessible only by administrators. `list` operations on these collections will be secured by checking the admin role (`exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`), ensuring that only authorized administrators can view these sensitive lists.

**Structural Segregation & Denormalization:**
*   `Order` entities are segregated into `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}` (for authenticated users) and `/guest_orders/{orderId}` (for non-authenticated users/admin-only management). This separates data with distinct security requirements.
*   The `Order` document itself, when stored under `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`, will include a `userId` field that matches the path, reinforcing ownership and authorization. This is a deliberate denormalization to achieve authorization independence. All wildcards are descriptive, such as `{userId}` and `{orderId}`, enhancing clarity and debuggability.
